generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACAutomationController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback controller for automated HVAC adjustments based on real-time occupancy and environmental data from the Digital Twin, enabling adaptive energy optimization."
]

instance OccupancyBehaviorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "System for dynamically adjusting building systems (e.g., lighting, HVAC) based on real-time occupancy patterns detected through sensor networks."
]

instance EnergySavingProtocolActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Rule-based actuator implementing energy-saving protocols derived from model-driven engineering frameworks for automated operational optimization."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity distributed temperature sensor network for real-time environmental monitoring across building zones."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity distributed humidity sensor network for real-time environmental monitoring across building zones."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity distributed occupancy sensor network (PIR, thermal cameras) for real-time monitoring of building usage patterns."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance AirQualitySensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity distributed air quality sensor network (CO2, VOCs, particulate matter) for real-time environmental monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringSensors : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity structural health monitoring sensors (vibration, strain gauges) for real-time building integrity assessment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorNetwork
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensorNetwork
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensorNetwork
]

instance AirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualitySensorNetwork
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitoringSensors
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity 3D Building Information Model aligned with IFC/COBie standards, encapsulating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building assets."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, DittoEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance BehavioralSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model representing dynamic behaviors of building systems (HVAC performance, structural response) derived from parametric simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelicaSimulatorEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven analytical model using statistical methods (control charts, moving averages) for predictive analytics and anomaly detection in building operations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ApacheKafkaEnabler, DittoEnabler
]

instance TimeSeriesEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time time-series data of environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, air quality) stored in TimescaleDB for trend analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, AirQualityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsModel
]

instance OccupancyPatternData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of occupancy patterns and behavior analytics derived from sensor networks for predictive maintenance and space utilization optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoEnabler, BehavioralSimulationModel
]

instance StructuralHealthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series structural health monitoring data for condition assessment and predictive maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge-to-cloud data ingestion layer standardizing IoT device connectivity via MQTT/HTTP protocols for real-time sensor data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance DittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Device twin management system providing real-time synchronization between physical assets and digital representations with event-driven updates."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAutomationService, VisualizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity 3D visualization engine enabling immersive, interactive 3D exploration of building states with real-time physics simulations and AR/VR support."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, ImmersiveCollaborationService
]

instance ModelicaSimulatorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Multi-physics simulation engine (Dymola) for modeling thermal, structural, and fluid dynamics behaviors of building systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, ControlAutomationService
]

instance ApacheKafkaEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-throughput event streaming platform enabling real-time data processing and synchronization between sensors, edge nodes, and the Digital Twin core."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, DataManagementService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for querying historical trends and supporting predictive analytics workflows."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataManagementService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseModelingFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework (EMF/Acceleo) for automated code generation from UML/SysML models in the Digital Twin pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDrivenEngineeringService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and streaming analytics service providing immediate condition assessment of building operations through real-time dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalConditionInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Time-series forecasting and anomaly detection service generating predictive insights for operational metrics and maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyForecastInsight, StructuralConditionAlertInsight
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback service enabling adaptive control of building systems through rule-based automation and model-driven engineering."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACOptimizationAction, EnergySavingProtocolAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization service providing real-time exploration of building states through Unreal Engine interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateVisualizationInsight
]

instance ImmersiveCollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Augmented Reality service enabling virtual walkthroughs and interactive data exploration for collaborative decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides ARMaintenanceGuidanceAction
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated model generation service translating BIM data into executable simulation models using domain-specific languages."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelConsistencyInsight
]

instance DataManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Time-series data storage and retrieval service optimized for querying historical trends and supporting predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance EnvironmentalConditionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental condition assessment including temperature, humidity, and air quality metrics for operational monitoring."
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy pattern analysis for space utilization optimization and predictive maintenance scheduling."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive forecast of energy consumption patterns and efficiency metrics for operational optimization."
]

instance StructuralConditionAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection alerts for structural health monitoring indicating potential integrity issues."
]

instance BuildingStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of current building state enabling immersive exploration of operational conditions."
]

instance ModelConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of consistency between physical asset models and digital twin representations through automated validation."
]

instance DataQualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of data quality metrics ensuring reliable inputs for predictive analytics and decision-making."
]

instance HVACOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of HVAC systems based on real-time occupancy and environmental data to optimize energy performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergySavingProtocolAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of predefined energy-saving protocols derived from model-driven engineering frameworks."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ARMaintenanceGuidanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Augmented reality overlay providing predictive maintenance alerts and guidance directly on physical assets."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "LEED Platinum-certified academic building serving as a research-oriented living lab for interdisciplinary collaboration."
    base:contains BIMGeometricModel, BehavioralSimulationModel, TemperatureSensorNetwork, HumiditySensorNetwork,
                 OccupancySensorNetwork, AirQualitySensorNetwork, StructuralHealthMonitoringSensors
]

// C5: Components
instance BuildingManagementSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Automated control system integrating sensor data and enabling real-time building operations management."
]

instance InteractiveDigitalInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User-facing digital interface enabling research engagement and operational testing through interactive visualization."
]

instance ResearchCollaborationPlatform : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Open-access data platform facilitating integration with external stakeholders for interdisciplinary research collaboration."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin framework is designed to enable **bidirectional communication** between the digital model and physical building systems, primarily for **real-time monitoring and control**. The framework supports **interactive visualization** and **control semantics** tailored to operational requirements, allowing **Facility Managers** to interact with the deployed DT for dynamic adjustments. While the documents emphasize the **bidirectional nature** of the interaction (e.g., via the TwinLight project context implied in the framework’s design), specific details such as **control message formats**, **validation/authentication mechanisms**, or **feedback systems** are not explicitly described. The framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** facilitate custom service integration, enabling tailored control logic for physical assets. However, no mention of specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, BACnet), command types, or conflict resolution strategies is provided in the documents.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system supports **real-time data exchange capabilities**, ensuring continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts with **low-latency requirements** to maintain operational relevance. The framework emphasizes **dynamic operational needs** over static BIM representations, enabling time scales for different services to adapt to **runtime configuration management**—allowing adjustments without full redeployment. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz or ms intervals) are not detailed, the system prioritizes **temporal granularity** sufficient for **immersive interactive visualization** and **facility monitoring/control**, aligning with the AECO domain’s demand for near-instantaneous feedback loops. Latency constraints are implicitly addressed via modular architecture and standardized extension points to accommodate evolving operational requirements without compromising temporal fidelity."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke architecture**, where the DT Framework acts as the **orchestration core** coordinating roles (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and system components. The constellation is governed by **coordinated roles and system components** as illustrated in Figure 1, with the hub facilitating:
  - **Bidirectional data exchange** between physical assets and digital models.
  - **Modular service integration** via standardized extension points for custom analytics.
  - **Runtime configuration management** to dynamically adjust operational workflows without redeployment.
  The system’s **nonfunctional requirements (NF_X)** explicitly address **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, **scalability** (from components to entire infrastructure), **maintainability** (low-overhead updates), and **accessibility** (user-friendly interfaces). Resource management is implied through the framework’s adaptability, though specific orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) are not detailed beyond the modular design."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this framework spans **five core phases**, each addressing the transition from static BIM representations to dynamic, operational DTs:

1. **Device Modeling**
   - Building Control Engineers embed **domain-specific control semantics** (sensors, actuators, controllers) directly into BIM models, overcoming BIM’s static limitations. This phase leverages **BIM enrichment** to create a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. The process involves **model-driven engineering (MDE) principles** to define device configurations and control logic within the BIM environment.

2. **Model Transformation**
   - The framework employs **model-to-model (M2M) and model-to-text (M2T) transformations** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models. These transformations generate **deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention, ensuring consistency between the physical and digital domains. The output includes **semantic annotations** for interoperability and **standardized data schemas** for runtime deployment.

3. **Runtime Deployment**
   - The DT is deployed as a **modular, real-time synchronized system** between physical infrastructure and its digital counterpart. This phase integrates **standardized extension points** (e.g., Eclipse Hono and Ditto) to enable seamless data exchange. The framework supports **continuous synchronization** via real-time APIs, ensuring operational coherence between the twin and physical assets.

4. **DT Extension**
   - DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via the framework’s modular architecture. This phase enables **iterative improvement** of control policies (e.g., via the BOREALIS project) and supports **analytical extensions** (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization). Extensions are version-controlled and deployed dynamically without disrupting core DT operations.

5. **Lifecycle Governance**
   - Governance is achieved through **semantic versioning** of DT artifacts, ensuring traceability and consistency across phases. The framework includes **role-based access control** (aligned with Table I roles) to manage permissions for Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers. Additionally, **audit logs** and **change impact analysis** tools are embedded to monitor modifications and ensure compliance with operational requirements.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through **semantic integration** and **real-time connectivity**, ensuring alignment between physical assets and digital representations. Key considerations include:
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements**: High fidelity is achieved via **BIM’s semantically rich models**, which incorporate **geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics** of assets, validated against industry standards (e.g., IFC, COBie). The system prioritizes **operational control DTs**, where fidelity is critical for decision-making (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization).
  - **Validation and Verification Methods**: Implicitly supported by **model-driven deployment artifacts** (e.g., **R2: automated deployment process**), which ensure reproducibility and consistency. **Design Science Research (DSR)** methodologies are applied to validate the framework’s ability to operationalize DTs in AECO contexts.
  - **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**: While not explicitly quantified, uncertainties arise from **sensor noise, model simplifications, and temporal delays** in real-time data integration. Mitigation strategies include **real-time synchronization (Eclipse Hono + Ditto)** and **ontological mappings** to reduce semantic discrepancies.
  - **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**: The system’s **real-time data integration** (R3) enables continuous comparison between physical and digital states, facilitating **automated anomaly detection** (e.g., via Ditto’s device twin reconciliation). However, specific error correction protocols (e.g., feedback loops, adaptive models) are not detailed.
  - **Quality Assurance Processes**: Quality is ensured through **model-driven automation**, which standardizes deployment workflows and reduces manual intervention (R2). Additionally, the **modular architecture** (R4) allows for **service-level quality checks** at extension points, though formalized QA procedures (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are not referenced."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is characterized by a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, ensuring **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** (NF_X). The framework integrates **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for **real-time data exchange**, though specific communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, CoAP, OPC UA), network topology (e.g., hybrid edge-cloud), or latency requirements are not detailed. The system prioritizes **scalability** (NF_X) and **reliability** through its **runtime configuration management**, enabling adjustments without full redeployment. Security protocols for network communication are implied via the framework’s **interoperability requirements**, but no explicit mention of encryption standards, firewalls, or redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover systems) is present. The infrastructure supports **low-overhead updates** for maintainability, ensuring seamless integration of new services or analytics.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is governed by a **model-driven deployment framework**, enabling scalable DT generation from **enriched BIM models**. The system emphasizes **scalability** (NF_X) and **maintainability** through **low-overhead updates**, though specific hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise) or deployment models (e.g., hybrid) are not explicitly mentioned. The framework leverages **runtime configuration management** to adapt to operational requirements without redeployment, ensuring flexibility. **Availability and reliability** are addressed via the framework’s **systematic foundation**, which bridges static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs. However, details such as **computational/storage requirements**, **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., auto-scaling), or **zero-downtime update capabilities** are not described in the provided documents.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The framework integrates **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** for **real-time data exchange**, enabling seamless interoperability with external systems. While the document does not explicitly list **OPC UA** or other protocols, it emphasizes **standardized extension points** and **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as core requirements. Dependencies on external systems are managed through the **centralized hub’s bidirectional data exchange**, though specific integration patterns (e.g., with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems) are not detailed."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **standardized integration frameworks** (e.g., Eclipse Hono, Ditto) for interoperability between BIM models and DT artifacts. While specific industry standards (e.g., IFC4, BACnet, OPC UA) are not explicitly listed, the document emphasizes:
- **BIM as the foundational standard** for device modeling and control logic definition.
- **Modular architecture** with standardized extension points to support third-party integrations.
- **Real-time data exchange protocols** (implied via Eclipse Hono and Ditto) for synchronization between physical and digital twins.

No explicit mention of compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 23247, GDPR, NIST SP 800-53) or protocol versions is provided. The framework’s interoperability is primarily enabled by **MDE-based transformations** and **standardized DT deployment artifacts**, ensuring consistency across the lifecycle.
"
]
